expectations.text: Output covers all three tables: USER_PROFILES, TXN_LEDGER, and MODEL_SCORES
expectations.evidence: All three tables covered with individual sections. Each includes modern equivalent, team owner, and contact channel.

expectations.text: Output calls out PII-sensitive columns (user_token at minimum)
expectations.evidence: PII warnings present: user_token flagged as PII, identity tables noted as DSL3, APP_CASH_PII database naming called out explicitly.

expectations.text: Output explains what each table contains in plain English
expectations.evidence: Each table has a plain-English summary: USER_PROFILES (identity data), TXN_LEDGER (banking transactions), MODEL_SCORES (fraud ML scores).

expectations.text: Output includes a cross-table synthesis explaining how they relate
expectations.evidence: Cross-table synthesis present explaining the JOIN logic and what the overall query is trying to do (fraud score enrichment of user+txn data).

expectations.text: Output includes ownership or team context for at least one table
expectations.evidence: All three tables have team context: Identity (#cash-identity-data-questions), Payments (#cash-finplat-help), Risk ML (#model-manager, #cash-data-etl).

expectations.text: Output includes Flags & Warnings with PII-related cautions
expectations.evidence: Flags section covers: wrong database prefix (CASH_APP vs APP_CASH), PII in user_token, DSL3 sensitivity, fraud_v3 model name not found.
